# PF2e — Wealth by Level & Treasure Distribution

Mechanical reference for the AI GM's loot generation. Ensures balanced item and gold distribution.

---

## Character Wealth by Level (Permanent Items)

Expected permanent items a character should have at each level. This is cumulative — by level 5, a character should possess ALL items listed for levels 1-5.

| Level | Permanent Items Gained | Typical Item Types |
|-------|----------------------|-------------------|
| 1 | 1 × 1st-level item | armor/weapon potency or skill item |
| 2 | 1 × 2nd-level item | +1 weapon or +1 armor |
| 3 | 1 × 3rd-level item | skill item or talisman |
| 4 | 1 × 4th-level item | +1 striking weapon |
| 5 | 1 × 5th-level item | +1 resilient armor |
| 6 | 1 × 6th-level item | skill or utility item |
| 7 | 1 × 7th-level item | property rune (1st) |
| 8 | 1 × 8th-level item | +1 striking weapon (backup) |
| 9 | 1 × 9th-level item | utility item |
| 10 | 1 × 10th-level item | +1 resilient armor upgrade or +2 striking weapon |
| 11 | 1 × 11th-level item | +2 resilient armor |
| 12 | 1 × 12th-level item | +2 greater striking weapon |
| 13 | 1 × 13th-level item | property rune (2nd) |
| 14 | 1 × 14th-level item | +2 greater resilient armor |
| 15 | 1 × 15th-level item | utility/apex item |
| 16 | 1 × 16th-level item | +3 greater striking weapon |
| 17 | 1 × 17th-level item | apex item (if not already) |
| 18 | 1 × 18th-level item | +3 greater resilient armor |
| 19 | 1 × 19th-level item | major property rune |
| 20 | 1 × 20th-level item | legendary item |

---

## Currency by Level

Loose currency (gp, sp, etc.) a character should have accumulated at each level. This is total expected wealth in currency, NOT per session.

| Level | Total Currency (gp) | Consumables Budget (gp) |
|-------|-------------------|------------------------|
| 1 | 15 | 10 |
| 2 | 30 | 18 |
| 3 | 50 | 25 |
| 4 | 80 | 36 |
| 5 | 125 | 50 |
| 6 | 200 | 70 |
| 7 | 300 | 100 |
| 8 | 450 | 135 |
| 9 | 650 | 200 |
| 10 | 1,000 | 300 |
| 11 | 1,400 | 400 |
| 12 | 2,000 | 600 |
| 13 | 3,000 | 850 |
| 14 | 4,500 | 1,250 |
| 15 | 6,500 | 1,800 |
| 16 | 10,000 | 2,800 |
| 17 | 15,000 | 4,000 |
| 18 | 24,000 | 6,500 |
| 19 | 36,000 | 10,000 |
| 20 | 55,000 | 15,000 |

---

## Treasure by Encounter Difficulty

How much treasure to award per encounter, scaled by party-level equivalent.

### Party Treasure per Level (4 players, full adventuring level)

| Level | Total Treasure Value (gp) | Permanent Items | Consumables | Currency |
|-------|--------------------------|-----------------|-------------|----------|
| 1 | 175 | 2 × 1st | 2 × 1st-level consumables | 40 gp |
| 2 | 300 | 2 × 2nd | 2 × 2nd | 70 gp |
| 3 | 500 | 2 × 3rd | 2 × 3rd | 120 gp |
| 4 | 850 | 2 × 4th | 2 × 4th | 200 gp |
| 5 | 1,350 | 2 × 5th | 2 × 5th | 320 gp |
| 6 | 2,000 | 2 × 6th | 2 × 6th | 500 gp |
| 7 | 2,900 | 2 × 7th | 2 × 7th | 720 gp |
| 8 | 4,000 | 2 × 8th | 2 × 8th | 1,000 gp |
| 9 | 5,700 | 2 × 9th | 2 × 9th | 1,400 gp |
| 10 | 8,000 | 2 × 10th | 2 × 10th | 2,000 gp |
| 11 | 11,500 | 2 × 11th | 2 × 11th | 2,800 gp |
| 12 | 16,500 | 2 × 12th | 2 × 12th | 4,000 gp |
| 13 | 25,000 | 2 × 13th | 2 × 13th | 6,000 gp |
| 14 | 36,000 | 2 × 14th | 2 × 14th | 9,000 gp |
| 15 | 54,500 | 2 × 15th | 2 × 15th | 13,000 gp |
| 16 | 82,000 | 2 × 16th | 2 × 16th | 20,000 gp |
| 17 | 128,000 | 2 × 17th | 2 × 17th | 30,000 gp |
| 18 | 208,000 | 2 × 18th | 2 × 18th | 48,000 gp |
| 19 | 355,000 | 2 × 19th | 2 × 19th | 80,000 gp |
| 20 | 490,000 | 2 × 20th | 2 × 20th | 140,000 gp |

### Solo Player Adjustment
For a single-player game (this system), divide party treasure by 4, then adjust:
- **Permanent items**: Award 1 item per level instead of 2 (since 1 character)
- **Consumables**: Award 1 consumable per level (reduced proportionally)
- **Currency**: Divide by 4 (one player's share)

---

## Encounter Budget (XP)

XP determines encounter difficulty. The party earns XP per creature defeated.

### XP by Creature Level Relative to Party

| Creature Level vs Party | XP per Creature |
|------------------------|----------------|
| Party Level -4 | 10 XP |
| Party Level -3 | 15 XP |
| Party Level -2 | 20 XP |
| Party Level -1 | 30 XP |
| Party Level +0 | 40 XP |
| Party Level +1 | 60 XP |
| Party Level +2 | 80 XP |
| Party Level +3 | 120 XP |
| Party Level +4 | 160 XP |

### Encounter Difficulty Thresholds (4-player party)

| Difficulty | Total XP Budget | Character Adjustment |
|-----------|----------------|---------------------|
| **Trivial** | 40 XP or less | -10 XP per missing character |
| **Low** | 60 XP | -15 XP per missing character |
| **Moderate** | 80 XP | -20 XP per missing character |
| **Severe** | 120 XP | -30 XP per missing character |
| **Extreme** | 160 XP | -40 XP per missing character |

### Solo Player Encounter Budget

For a single-player game, the party is 1 character (3 fewer than standard):

| Difficulty | XP Budget (1 player) |
|-----------|---------------------|
| **Trivial** | 10 XP (single PL-4 creature) |
| **Low** | 15 XP (single PL-3 creature) |
| **Moderate** | 20 XP (single PL-2 creature) |
| **Severe** | 30 XP (single PL-1 creature) |
| **Extreme** | 40 XP (single PL+0 creature) |

> **AI GM Note:** A solo character fighting a creature of equal level is already an EXTREME encounter. The AI GM should typically build encounters around PL-2 to PL+0 creatures. Boss fights should rarely exceed PL+1 without giving the player extra resources (NPC ally, environmental advantage, consumables).

---

## Consumable Types & Pricing

Consumable items are single-use and priced lower than permanent items.

| Consumable Level | Price (gp) | Example Types |
|-----------------|-----------|---------------|
| 1 | 3-4 | lesser healing potion, minor talisman |
| 2 | 5-7 | +1 ammunition (3), lesser antidote |
| 3 | 8-12 | lesser antiplague, feather token |
| 4 | 15-20 | moderate healing potion |
| 5 | 25-30 | lesser darkvision elixir |
| 6 | 40-50 | moderate antidote, scroll (2nd) |
| 7 | 55-70 | moderate bomb |
| 8 | 75-100 | moderate healing potion |
| 9 | 110-150 | scroll (3rd), greater talisman |
| 10 | 155-200 | greater bomb |
| 11 | 225-300 | greater healing potion |
| 12 | 325-400 | scroll (4th) |
| 13 | 450-600 | greater antidote |
| 14 | 650-900 | scroll (5th) |
| 15 | 975-1,300 | major bomb |
| 16 | 1,400-1,750 | scroll (6th) |
| 17 | 2,100-2,500 | major healing potion |
| 18 | 3,000-4,000 | scroll (7th) |
| 19 | 5,000-8,000 | scroll (8th) |
| 20 | 10,000-14,000 | scroll (9th) |

---

## Treasure Distribution Guidelines for AI GM

### Per-Session Loot Pacing
- A "level" of content is typically 12–14 encounters spread across multiple sessions
- Award treasure after roughly every 3-4 encounters
- Drip-feed currency and consumables; save permanent items for milestone moments

### Loot Placement Rules
1. **Contextual items**: Defeated enemies should carry level-appropriate gear
2. **Hidden treasure**: Perception/Thievery DCs to find should match encounter level
3. **Shop availability**: Limited by settlement size (see golarion-cities.md)
4. **Crafting**: Characters can craft items up to their level if they have the formula and downtime
5. **Selling**: Characters get half price when selling items

### Solo Campaign Adjustments
- The single PC gets ALL the loot (no party split), so they accumulate faster
- Offset by reducing encounters-per-level slightly
- Give meaningful choices: "You find a +1 striking longsword. Your current shortsword +1 is lighter. Which do you keep?"
- Award sidekick NPCs separate (lesser) gear budgets if they exist
